meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no coding experience (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no coding experience. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Student Guide: Analytics Setup for No-Code AI Builders
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between no-code and professional engineering. Learn to set up analytics for your AI app using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert guide.
content.answer_block: You don't need a CS degree to track user behavior. By integrating event-based analytics into your AI stack using Claude Code and Antigravity, you transform raw interactions into actionable data. This setup provides the telemetry required to iterate faster, prove your product-market fit, and build a professional-grade portfolio.
content.intro:
The screen glows, your AI feature is live, but it's a black box. You’ve successfully navigated the no-code landscape, yet you’re flying blind without data. You can feel the tension—is anyone actually using your tool? Are they hitting the paywall, or are they getting stuck on the first prompt?

Stop guessing. We are going to bridge the gap between amateur builder and professional software engineer. By leveraging the automated orchestration of Antigravity and the reasoning power of Claude Code, we will inject enterprise-grade telemetry into your architecture. This isn't just about tracking clicks; it's about building the feedback loop that turns a student project into a scalable product.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, define what 'success' looks like for your user. Is it a completed generation, a shared link, or a recurring login?
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current AI application architecture and suggest 3 high-impact events to track that define user retention. Focus on LLM response satisfaction.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my project README and identify the top 3 user conversion points for analytics tracking.'

steps.title: Initialize the Analytics SDK
steps.description: Inject the analytics library into your core service file without manual boilerplate bloat.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, reusable analytics utility class that integrates with PostHog or Mixpanel, ensuring it handles asynchronous event queuing gracefully.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the analytics utility class in src/utils/analytics.ts and export the trackEvent function.'

steps.title: Instrument the AI Inference Pipeline
steps.description: Capture the metadata of your AI calls—latency, token usage, and prompt success—to optimize costs and performance.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a wrapper function for my OpenAI/Claude API calls that automatically logs request duration, token count, and error status as metadata.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Wrap the existing LLM service calls in src/services/ai.ts with the new analytics tracking utility.'

steps.title: Automate User Context Mapping
steps.description: Link events to unique user IDs to track the full user journey from onboarding to power-user status.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the authentication flow to ensure every analytics event captures the current user's session ID and subscription tier automatically.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Update the auth provider to set the user context in the global analytics state.'

steps.title: Validate Event Streams
steps.description: Verify that your data is flowing correctly before deploying to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a test script that triggers mock events and validates that the analytics payload format matches the required schema.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a test file in tests/analytics.test.ts to verify event dispatching and payload structure.'

pro_tips: Always track 'time-to-first-value' (TTFV) as a primary metric; if your AI takes too long to respond, your churn rate will skyrocket.
Avoid tracking everything; focus on high-fidelity events that inform product decisions rather than vanity metrics like page views.
Implement 'Error Boundary' logging in your analytics to capture silent failures that don't crash the app but frustrate the user.
Use pseudonymous IDs for tracking to maintain privacy while still allowing for cohort analysis of your user base.
Set up automated alerts for when your AI inference latency crosses a specific threshold, effectively creating a health monitor for your app.
content.next_steps: Now that your analytics pipeline is live, set up a simple dashboard to visualize your user journey. Once you see the data, iterate on your prompt engineering based on where users drop off.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder with data-driven insights. Stop building in the dark and start engineering for impact. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
